description: Archive-extraction path traversal (Snyk 2018 — Zip Slip). Triggers when application unpacks attacker-controlled zip/tar/7z/rar/jar without canonicalising entry names — `../../../etc/cron.d/x` lands outside intended directory. Reference-only: requires upload-then-trigger sequence + post-extraction filesystem observation; operator drives via file_upload tool. Maps to OWASP WSTG 4.10.8 + PayloadsAllTheThings 'Insecure File Upload'.

zip_traversal_classic.description: Standard Zip Slip: zip entry name contains relative path. Affects Java (ZipInputStream pre-Java7u131), Python (zipfile pre-3.6.2 PR), Node (unzipper/adm-zip <0.5.0), Go (archive/zip pre-1.18), PHP (ZipArchive default).

probes.description: Build a zip with one harmless file but its entry name escapes the target dir. After upload + extract, check /tmp/swk-zipslip-{{marker}} exists OR app behaves as if file landed elsewhere.
Windows backslash variant — same idea, different path separator. Affects implementations that only filter forward slashes.

zip_overwrite_critical_files.description: Maximum-impact variant — overwrite app config / cron / startup scripts. Picks paths attacker can reasonably guess and that grant code execution on next service restart or cron tick.

probes.description: Drop a cron job — when cron service triggers, attacker payload runs as root
Per-user crontab overwrite — works if app runs as www-data and has write access to its own crontab
Drop SSH key into a known service account — persistent RCE

tar_extraction_traversal.description: Tar/Tar.gz variant: same idea but the tar archive format has its own name encoding. Some libraries pass strings to filesystem with no normalisation.

probes.description: Tar extraction with traversal name — Python tarfile pre-3.12 default is unsafe

rar_seven_z_traversal.description: RAR / 7z extraction variants. unrar (libarchive), 7-Zip CLI, p7zip-full — each has had CVE for traversal in entry name.

probes.description: 7z archive with traversal entry — affects p7zip pre-22.00 + .NET SharpCompress pre-0.30.0

symlink_in_archive.description: Archive contains a symlink pointing outside extraction directory + a regular file at the symlink's name. Two-stage attack: first extract creates symlink, second extract writes through symlink to attacker-controlled path. Affects libraries that don't follow --no-symlinks default.

probes.description: Two-entry exploit: symlink first, file second. On extract, second entry overwrites cron.d via symlink.

jar_zip_polyglot.description: Many Java apps re-extract uploaded .jar files for plugin/extension hot-load. Zip Slip in a .jar with `Class-Path` manifest pointing at a sibling .jar can pull-in attacker code.

probes.description: Plugin/extension loader pulls referenced jar in turn

post_extract_observability.description: Detection strategy when filesystem observation isn't possible (typical bug-bounty target). Indicators of successful extraction-traversal.

probes.description: (1) App responds with file-not-found before any other behavior — extraction happened. (2) Service starts behaving differently after extraction — file landed in classpath / autoload dir. (3) Subsequent request to attacker-guessed path returns the planted content.
